The Point of No Return (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 3
Speaker 1: Alright, guys, we are back for Themeplate 13, and this is Te Point of No Return, and
what we're doing here is we're highlighting the cost of going backwards, so we're
detailing that burning crash in the rear-view, painting a picture that the prospect will
want to continue driving away from as far and fast as possible.
So this is the classic risk revealing, really bringing that intimacy with the stakes, and
creating more tension in going backwards that moving forwards. So, in short, this is
what the container looks like. You will first start off by listing out what tomorrow will
hold for them, independent of your product, whether or not you buy... That's what
you're looking for in step one.
You'll then future-paste what success using your product can look like, and then you'll
show and contrast that with the burning crash in the rear-view, which will include your
moment with the highest tension. Then, you'll briefly restate and really reaffirm, re-
contextualize those re-frames, a lot of re's there, and sacred cows from the prelaunch.
So we're reinforcing them here, and then invite them to move forward, continue driving
on with your program and lead into your call to action.
So, it's a short one, it's a good one. Let's dive into the example.
Burning Man versus Broken Funnels. The doors to Ten-X launches are now open, but
whether or not you join you're still going to be building funnels, you're still gonna be
launching new online courses, masterminds, and programs, you're still gonna be
manning the controls of your convert kit, Melcham, drip infusion software, whatever the
heck you're using to send out messages that get people to buy.
The growth of your online business, the number of employees you can bring in to do the
stuff you're not great at, and the number of weeks you can take off this year to do the
things you swore you'd finally cross off your bucket list, like that trip to Bali, hiking
Machu Pichu, or doing whatever the hell people do at Burning Man, will all be a result of
what happened in the moment after you hit send.
So we're tying all those future-paste outcomes, the ones that are on their radar, and
linking that to that action of what happens when you hit send. No pressure, but here's
where you have a choice.
We can pull a good old-fashioned Men In Black memory swipe on you and erase
everything you've learned over the last seven days, that way you can confidently dig
your head back into the proverbial sand and return to the cozy and familiar narrative
that all I need is the latest funnel. Who cares about that pesky execution thing?
Even if I need to execute, I'll just throw a bone to one of those copywriters clamoring for
work. They're all super cheap and affordable. So really bringing those sacred cows that
we worked on in the prelaunch back into the fold. Uh-Oh! Guess I was wrong. It's cool. I
got this. I'll put some bulletproof coffee in the pots, swipe a bit of this, a bit of that, and
fire it off.

The Point of No Return (Completed 05/08/18)

Because I have this super fancy funnel and some ninja Facebook retargeting stuff rolling
out, the copy thing can't be that important.
So, once again, that burning fire, and then, "Oh crap, why isn't this converting? My
Facebook ads must be broken! My pixel must not be firing correctly. Wait, I don't even
know what a pixel is, but I heard it was important.
Or maybe I should have done a webinar! Ah! All good, I'm just gonna fail forward. I'll buy
that new program, or funnel course or whatever, and crush it next time.
So really, kind of reflecting back that absurd narrative that is so common for this market,
and given them a chance to see, oh, that is a train wreck. That is a burning fire. I don't
want that. I'm gonna drive as far away from that inner monologue as possible.
And speaking about an inner monologue, that's a great way to dial up specificity. So
when you are writing these types of bullets, feel free to play around with an inner
monologue. Writing it from the perspective of what your prospect would be thinking or
feeling, in either real time or a future-paste time. That's an extra little writing tip.
Painful. Glad we're graduating out of that. Here's your second option. So here's where
we contrast it, and give them a chance to really cash in on this. They have been
exclusively empowered, and they see a new way. You can leverage the hard-earned
insights you've integrated into your synapses over this past week. That execution is
everything, and that funnel will only be as strong as the words that fill it.
But you, and you alone, are responsible for executing. Sure there's a time and place to
bring on a copywriter, but just like some of the world's top entrepreneurs still write
their own, it's your job, as someone who's in charge of bringing in profits, to take full
ownership and control, that, hey, it's not as hard as it looks, and wow! I don't even need
to memorize formulas, shoehorn my words into rigid templates, or hand-write old sales
letters until my wrist hurts before being confident enough to knock out high-converting
copy that makes the cash register ring.
So, we're really not adding new arguments here. We're restating the beliefs and
arguments that were presented in the prelaunch, except now it's re-contextualized with
our program. I can just get my Ten-X launch's login info, which need minimum viable
mastery over this launch copy thing, and needs one of 20-plus proven themeplates to
fuel my highest conversions yet.
Yep, that's an option, and arguably the only one you will... Only one. Arguably the only
one that will have you hiking your version of Machu Pichu by quarter's end to celebrate
your best launch yet, 'cause whether or not you join Ten-X launches, you're in the online
business game for the long haul.
Time to play it with a stacked deck. This is just showing that we are entering their reality
and their goals. We're not changing it, we're just showing that our program, and the
